chips.description: Mesquite Bbq chips celebrate today's Australia Day spirit, merging smoky flavors with Aussie pride. These barbecue-flavored snacks capture the essence of a classic Australian feast, perfect for today's celebration.
chips.name: Mesquite Bbq

chips.description: Today marks the independence of Nauru from Australia in 1968. Join in the celebration with our Vinegar Cheddar chips - their tangy flavor mirrors the spirit of freedom and zest that marks this day of pride.
chips.name: Vinegar Cheddar

AU.fact: Australia has unique native ingredients like bush tomato and wattleseed

BE.fact: Belgium claims to have invented french fries, not France

BO.fact: Bolivia grows over 200 varieties of native potatoes

BR.fact: Cassava originated in the Amazon Basin and is a staple across tropical regions

CA.fact: Canada produces 80% of the world's maple syrup
CA.name: Canada

CH.fact: Swiss Alps herbs include unique varieties found only at high altitudes
CH.name: Switzerland

chips.description: Today's Chip of the Day is 'Barbecue Smoky Mustard', paying tribute to Day of the Young Combatant (Chile).
chips.name: Barbecue Smoky Mustard

CL.fact: Chile's long coastline influences its unique seafood-potato combinations
CL.name: Chile

chips.description: Today, as we mark Arbor Day (China), embrace nature's bounty with our Lime chips.
chips.name: Lime

chips.description: Today's culinary tribute celebrates China's Cold Food Festival. Scallion Pancake chips offer a savory blend that mirrors ancient recipes, perfect for those seeking flavors from centuries past.

DE.fact: Germany is one of Europe's largest potato producers

chips.description: Today, Finland celebrates Runeberg Day. Join in the festivities with Garlic Rye chips, infusing classic Finnish flavors into each crispy bite - the perfect blend for honoring this national tradition.
chips.name: Garlic Rye

chips.description: Today, Finland celebrates its rich cultural heritage on Kalevala Day. Embrace this tradition with our Rye Savory chips, a delightful nod to Finnish flavors and history.
chips.name: Rye Savory

FI.fact: Finnish cuisine emphasizes local, foraged ingredients
FI.name: Finland

FR.fact: Herbes de Provence is a blend of dried herbs typical of southeastern France
FR.name: France

chips.description: Today marks Grenada's independence from the United Kingdom. Embrace freedom and flavor with our Parmesan Herb chips, celebrating diversity through every bite.
chips.name: Parmesan Herb

chips.description: Truffle Salt, today's ode to Saint Lucia's independence from the UK.
chips.name: Truffle Salt

GB.fact: The UK calls them 'crisps' - the term 'chips' refers to french fries
GB.name: United Kingdom

chips.description: Jerk, today's Chip of the Day, pays homage to Ghana's independence journey from Britain. Each crisp bite mirrors the bold flavors that marked this significant moment in history, celebrating freedom and heritage.
chips.name: Jerk

GH.fact: Ghana's plantain chips are a beloved street food
GH.name: Ghana

GR.fact: Greek olive oil production dates back over 4,000 years

ID.fact: Indonesia is the world's largest producer of prawn crackers (krupuk)
ID.name: Indonesia

IE.fact: The potato became central to Irish cuisine after its introduction from South America
IE.name: Ireland

chips.description: Today, we celebrate India's Propose Day with Masala's vibrant flavors. Infused with the essence of traditional spices and a dash of love, these chips bring the heart of Indian cuisine to your celebration.
chips.name: Masala

IN.fact: India has over 100 varieties of masala spice blends across regions
IN.name: India

IR.fact: Iran produces 90% of the world's saffron
IR.name: Iran

chips.description: Celebrating Dialect Day from the Amami Islands of Japan, these Jalapeno chips bridge traditional island flavors with spicy zest.
chips.name: Jalapeno

chips.description: Today, honor Japan's Kitano Baika-sai "Plum Blossom Festival". Experience Cherry Sweet, Cherry chips - their delicate flavor pays tribute to this blooming beauty, just like spring arrives in Kyoto.
chips.name: Cherry Sweet, Cherry

chips.description: On this day, we honor Buddha's enlightenment journey. Embrace the essence of wisdom and balance with our Roots Lotus Wasabi chipsspicy yet harmonious, just like the path to inner peace.

JP.fact: Japan has unique chip flavors like sakura and matcha
JP.name: Japan

KR.fact: Korean cuisine features unique fermented ingredients like gochujang
KR.name: South Korea

chips.description: Today, Kuwait celebrates Liberation Day from Iraqi occupation. Savor the freedom and joy of this historic event with our Restaurant chips, offering the bold flavors and rich tastes that embody the spirit of independence.

KW.fact: Kuwaiti cuisine features Middle Eastern spice traditions
KW.name: Kuwait

chips.description: Chipotle Bbq Oil - Today is Constitution Day, marking the beginning of celebrating democracy in Mexico.
chips.name: Chipotle Bbq Oil

chips.description: Marking an important day for Mexico's military tradition, our Taco chips offer a burst of authentic Mexican flavors.

MX.fact: Corn was first domesticated in Mexico over 9,000 years ago
MX.name: Mexico

NG.fact: Nigeria is the world's largest producer of yams
NG.name: Nigeria

NL.fact: The Dutch have a long history with potatoes since the 17th century
NL.name: Netherlands

NO.fact: Norwegian cuisine features unique preservation traditions
NO.name: Norway

NZ.fact: New Zealand cuisine blends Māori traditions with global influences
NZ.name: New Zealand

PE.fact: Peru is the birthplace of the potato, cultivated since 8000 BC with over 3,000 varieties
PE.name: Peru

PH.fact: The Philippines has unique chip varieties made from banana and cassava
PH.name: Philippines

SE.fact: Sweden has unique potato varieties adapted to northern climates
SE.name: Sweden

SG.fact: Singapore's food culture blends Chinese, Malay, Indian, and Western influences
SG.name: Singapore

chips.description: Lemon Basil Chip celebrates today's Children's Day spirit from Thailand. A vibrant mix of citrus freshness and aromatic basil, perfect for this joyful celebration.
chips.name: Lemon Basil

chips.description: Today marks National Elephant Day in Thailand, where conservation efforts are highlighted.
chips.name: Thai Sweet Chilli

chips.description: Today marks Chakri Day, celebrating the founding of the Chakri dynasty. Revel in Thai heritage with Sour Cream Onion, blending creamy richness and tangy onion flavors for an exquisite taste experience.
chips.name: Sour Cream Onion

TH.fact: Thai cuisine balances sweet, sour, salty, and spicy flavors
TH.name: Thailand

TR.fact: Turkish cuisine bridges European and Asian culinary traditions
TR.name: Turkey

chips.description: Tomato Basil - Today's flavor takes us on an Italian journey to celebrate Ratification Day.
chips.name: Tomato Basil

US.fact: The potato chip was invented in Saratoga Springs, NY in 1853
US.name: United States

cassava.description: Also known as yuca or manioc, cassava originated in South America and spread globally via Portuguese traders.
cassava.name: Cassava (Manioc)

corn.description: Corn was first domesticated by indigenous peoples in Mexico from a wild grass called teosinte.
corn.name: Corn (Maize)

plantain.description: Plantains originated in Southeast Asia and spread to Africa and the Americas, becoming a cooking staple.
plantain.name: Plantain

potato.description: The humble potato originated in the high Andes of Peru and Bolivia. The Inca civilization cultivated over 3,000 varieties.
potato.name: Potato

rice.description: Rice was domesticated independently in China and India, forming the basis of Asian rice crackers.
rice.name: Rice

sweetpotato.description: Sweet potatoes originated in the Americas and spread to Polynesia before European contact.
sweetpotato.name: Sweet Potato

taro.description: Taro is one of the oldest cultivated plants, originating in Southeast Asia and spreading to the Pacific islands.
taro.name: Taro

yam.description: True yams are different from sweet potatoes. They originated in Africa and are a staple food across the continent.
yam.name: True Yam (Dioscorea)
